title: Grant Writer
description:
???? Grant Writer (Part-Time)

10–20 Hours Per Week | $5/hour

Opportunity to Transition to Full-Time After 6 Months (Performance-Based)



About ArtyHood:

ArtyHood creates vibrant, inclusive spaces where artists, businesses, and communities connect, grow, and thrive.



Through art shows, night markets, block parties, and cultural festivals, we help revitalize neighborhoods, support small businesses, and empower LGBTQ+, BIPOC, immigrant, and underrepresented artists.



We believe art is infrastructure. It drives economic opportunity. It strengthens community identity. It transforms public space into places of belonging.



To expand this impact, we’re looking for a mission-aligned Grant Writer who can help secure the funding that sustains and grows our programs.



The Role:

We’re seeking a resourceful, strategic, and detail-oriented fundraising professional who can identify, pursue, and manage grant opportunities that support ArtyHood’s:

- Public art markets and cultural festivals

- Artist workforce development initiatives

- Neighborhood revitalization efforts

- Community-based creative programs



This role is ideal for someone who understands that fundraising is not just about securing money — it’s about fueling cultural ecosystems and building long-term community infrastructure.



What You’ll Do:

- Research and identify grant opportunities from federal, state, local, and private funders

- Write compelling grant proposals, LOIs, and funding applications

- Develop budgets, impact statements, and project narratives

- Maintain a grants calendar to ensure timely submissions and reporting

- Track awarded grants, deliverables, and compliance requirements

- Collaborate with leadership to identify funding priorities and measurable outcomes

- Translate community impact into strong, data-informed storytelling

- Support fundraising strategies tied to events, activations, and expansion initiatives



Who You Are:

- Passionate about arts, culture, and community revitalization

- Mission-driven and aligned with equity and representation

- Experienced in grant writing, fundraising, or philanthropic partnerships

- A strong writer who can clearly communicate mission, metrics, and impact

- Highly organized and able to manage multiple deadlines

- Proactive and strategic in identifying funding opportunities

- Comfortable working in a growing, entrepreneurial nonprofit environment



Ideal Qualifications:

- Proven experience securing foundation or government grants

- Strong English writing and persuasive storytelling skills

- Familiarity with nonprofits, arts organizations, workforce development, or community programs

- Experience preparing program budgets and measurable impact frameworks

- Ability to manage documentation, deadlines, and reporting cycles independently



Growth Opportunity:

This is a part-time position (10–20 hours per week) at $5/hour, with the opportunity to transition into a full-time leadership role after 6 months based on performance and funding growth.



As ArtyHood expands, this role has the potential to grow into a Development Lead or Director-level position.



???? Important Application Instruction:

To ensure you’ve carefully read this entire job post, please begin your message with the exact phrase: “Creativity Builds Community”. Applications that do not start with this exact phrase will not be reviewed.



If you’re excited about using fundraising as a tool to empower artists, revitalize neighborhoods, and build inclusive creative ecosystems — we’d love to hear from you.



Please send your resume, cover letter (or message), and 1–2 relevant writing samples.

title: Virtual Assistant (Technical Content – Developer Infrastructure)
description:
**Remote (NYC Hours Required)**

**Full-Time (8 hrs/day)**

**5+ Years Professional Experience Required**



We’re hiring a high-caliber operator who can function as both:



1. A precision-focused Virtual Assistant

2. A strong technical content writer for a developer infrastructure product



If you are primarily a general admin VA, this role is not for you.



If you’ve written real documentation for APIs, deployment platforms, Kubernetes-based systems, or cloud infrastructure — keep reading.



---



## About Us



We’re a US-based startup building a developer-first deployment platform — similar to Vercel or Render — but designed for teams that want to deploy into their own cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure) or on-prem infrastructure.



Our users are engineers and platform teams. They care about correctness, security, performance, and infrastructure control.



We operate with high standards and low bureaucracy.



---



## What This Role Actually Is



You will:



* Bring structure and clarity to startup operations

* Turn raw engineering notes into production-grade documentation

* Help improve our internal systems across GitHub, Linear, Notion, and Slack

* Act as a force multiplier for founders and engineers



You will not:



* Just manage calendars

* Write fluffy marketing content

* Wait for detailed step-by-step instructions



We’re looking for someone who sees ambiguity and creates structure.



---



## Responsibilities



### Execution & Operational Rigor



* Own inbox and calendar coordination with precision

* Track issues and deliverables in Linear and GitHub

* Follow up consistently and close loops

* Create clean internal documentation in Notion

* Conduct structured research and produce concise summaries

* Identify process gaps and proactively improve them



### Technical Documentation (Developer-Facing)



* Write and maintain:



  * API documentation

  * Deployment workflows

  * Cloud setup guides (AWS/GCP/Azure)

  * Kubernetes-based deployment documentation

  * Self-hosting and infrastructure configuration guides

* Translate engineering discussions into clear, structured documentation

* Create realistic developer examples and workflows

* Improve clarity and usability of existing docs

* Use ChatGPT and Claude Code effectively — without relying on them for correctness



---



## Requirements (Non-Negotiable)



* 5+ years of professional experience

* Proven experience writing technical documentation for developer tools or infrastructure platforms

* Strong understanding of:



  * AWS, GCP, or Azure

  * CI/CD concepts

  * Containers (Docker fundamentals)

  * Infrastructure basics (networking, auth, environments)

* Ability to read and understand code (JavaScript, Python, or similar)

* Strong GitHub literacy (issues, PRs, repo structure)

* Excellent written English

* Highly organized and detail-oriented

* Must work NYC business hours



---



## Strong Advantage (Highly Valued)



* Practical familiarity with Kubernetes:



  * Understanding of pods, services, ingress, deployments

  * Helm or YAML-based configuration

  * K8s-based deployment patterns

* Experience documenting Kubernetes or cloud-native systems

* Experience in early-stage startups

* Experience supporting founders or senior operators

* Ability to test APIs and run CLI tools independently



Kubernetes familiarity is a major plus, especially given the nature of our platform.



---



## What “Great” Looks Like (90 Days)



* Our docs read like they were written by someone who understands infrastructure

* Engineers spend less time explaining basic cloud concepts

* Kubernetes and deployment flows are clearly documented

* Internal operations feel structured and predictable

* You operate with minimal oversight and high judgment



---



## Compensation



Competitive, based on experience.

Long-term role with meaningful ownership and leverage.



---



## Application Requirements



To be considered, include:



1. 2–3 examples of technical documentation you’ve written for developer tools or infrastructure products

2. A short explanation of your experience with cloud platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure)

3. Any experience working with Kubernetes (if applicable)

4. Your timezone and confirmation you can work NYC hours

5. A brief note explaining why you thrive in high-ownership environments



Applications without documentation samples will not be reviewed.

title: Finance Support Officer (with Xero experience required)
description:
About You

You’re someone who brings positive energy to your work and enjoys being part of a collaborative, purpose-driven team. You’re friendly, approachable, and people feel comfortable coming to you with questions.



You care about doing things properly, you’re happy to jump in, help out, and figure things out as you go. You enjoy ticking things off a list, improving how things work, and knowing your work genuinely helps others.



Details

Reports to: Management Accountant

Employment Status: Full-time, permanent employee

Working Hours: AU Standard business hours and additional time as required by the role



Role Purpose

The Finance Support Officer is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of core transactional finance processes, including accounts payable, bank reconciliations, corporate cards, payroll processing, and financial data administration. This role is critical to the smooth running of the finance function, ensuring accurate, timely, high-quality financial processing that supports strong reporting, compliance, and organizational decision-making.



Role Responsibilities

A. Accounts Payable:

Process all supplier invoices, employee & volunteer expense claims, ensuring correct coding, approvals, GST treatment, currency, and documentation.

Manage the AP inbox and triage queries.

Maintain accurate supplier records, including bank details and documentation checks.

Prepare AP batches in line with cut-off timelines and approval workflows.

Conduct first-pass AP review before Management Accountant review (accuracy, completeness, coding).

Reconcile AP accounts and investigate variances.



B. Accounts Receivable:

Prepare and send invoices to partners, funders, and internal stakeholders.

Maintain AR schedules and follow up on outstanding amounts.

Reconcile AR balances and ensure all receipts are allocated.



C. Corporate Credit Cards:

Manage card issuance, cancellations, limits, and user onboarding.

Review monthly card claims, ensuring receipts, coding, and notes are provided.

Follow up late claims and unresolved discrepancies.

Reconcile company card accounts and ensure accurate EOM processing.



D. Payroll:

Prepare and process fortnightly payroll in line with contracts, and organisational policies.

Maintain employee master data with Xero, ensuring accuracy of bank details, tax information, superannuation, and entitlements.

Process new starters, changes, terminations, and pay adjustments.

Reconcile payroll reports, checking for variance and anomalies.

Prepare workpapers for superannuation payments, payroll journals, and leave reconciliations.

Work with P&E to ensure payroll accuracy, and timely updates to employee records.



E. Bank Reconciliation:

Complete daily and monthly bank reconciliations across all accounts.

Investigate and resolve unreconciled transactions.

Ensure all receipts, payments, and transfers are accurately recorded.



F. End of Month Processes:

Draft prepayments, accruals, apportionments, and routine journals.

Prepare EOM workpapers to a standard ready for first review.

Ensure all transactional processing is complete before close deadlines.

Maintain high-quality data hygiene to support forecasting, reporting, and analysis.



G. Data Entry and Record Keeping:

Maintain accurate, organised financial files and documentation for audit readiness.

Ensure data entry is correct, complete, and aligned with financial policies and accounting standards.

Support system updates, automation improvements, and process changes.



H. General Administrative Support:

Provide customer-focused support to internal stakeholders on finance and payroll queries.

Assist the Finance Team during peak periods (audit, EOFY, EOM, etc).

Participate in continuous improvement projects.

Contribute to team discussions on process optimisation, automation, and better ways of working.



Key Skills and Experience

-Experience in a finance support, accounts, or payroll role.

-Solid understanding of accounts payable, accounts receivable, and payroll processes.

-Proficient with Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets.

-Experience with accounting software (Xero preferred).

-Strong numeracy and data-entry accuracy.

-Understanding of basic accounting and payroll principles.

-High attention to detail and ability to deliver consistent accuracy.

-Excellent time management and ability to work to tight deadlines.

-Strong communication and customer service skills.

-Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks.

-Comfortable with change, learning, and improving processes.

-Ability to handle confidential information with integrity and discretion.

-Certificate, Diploma, or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field

You care about doing things properly, you’re happy to jump in, help out, and figure things out as you go. You enjoy ticking things off a list, improving how things work, and knowing your work genuinely helps others.



Details

Reports to: Management Accountant

Employment Status: Full-time, permanent employee

Working Hours: AU Standard business hours and additional time as required by the role



Role Purpose

The Finance Support Officer is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of core transactional finance processes, including accounts payable, bank reconciliations, corporate cards, payroll processing, and financial data administration. This role is critical to the smooth running of the finance function, ensuring accurate, timely, high-quality financial processing that supports strong reporting, compliance, and organizational decision-making.



Role Responsibilities

A. Accounts Payable:

Process all supplier invoices, employee & volunteer expense claims, ensuring correct coding, approvals, GST treatment, currency, and documentation.

Manage the AP inbox and triage queries.

Maintain accurate supplier records, including bank details and documentation checks.

Prepare AP batches in line with cut-off timelines and approval workflows.

Conduct first-pass AP review before Management Accountant review (accuracy, completeness, coding).

Reconcile AP accounts and investigate variances.



B. Accounts Receivable:

Prepare and send invoices to partners, funders, and internal stakeholders.

Maintain AR schedules and follow up on outstanding amounts.

Reconcile AR balances and ensure all receipts are allocated.



C. Corporate Credit Cards:

Manage card issuance, cancellations, limits, and user onboarding.

Review monthly card claims, ensuring receipts, coding, and notes are provided.

Follow up late claims and unresolved discrepancies.

Reconcile company card accounts and ensure accurate EOM processing.



D. Payroll:

Prepare and process fortnightly payroll in line with contracts, and organisational policies.

Maintain employee master data with Xero, ensuring accuracy of bank details, tax information, superannuation, and entitlements.

Process new starters, changes, terminations, and pay adjustments.

Reconcile payroll reports, checking for variance and anomalies.

Prepare workpapers for superannuation payments, payroll journals, and leave reconciliations.

Work with P&E to ensure payroll accuracy, and timely updates to employee records.



E. Bank Reconciliation:

Complete daily and monthly bank reconciliations across all accounts.

Investigate and resolve unreconciled transactions.

Ensure all receipts, payments, and transfers are accurately recorded.



F. End of Month Processes:

Draft prepayments, accruals, apportionments, and routine journals.

Prepare EOM workpapers to a standard ready for first review.

Ensure all transactional processing is complete before close deadlines.

Maintain high-quality data hygiene to support forecasting, reporting, and analysis.



G. Data Entry and Record Keeping:

Maintain accurate, organised financial files and documentation for audit readiness.

Ensure data entry is correct, complete, and aligned with financial policies and accounting standards.

Support system updates, automation improvements, and process changes.



H. General Administrative Support:

Provide customer-focused support to internal stakeholders on finance and payroll queries.

Assist the Finance Team during peak periods (audit, EOFY, EOM, etc).

Participate in continuous improvement projects.

Contribute to team discussions on process optimisation, automation, and better ways of working.



Key Skills and Experience

-Experience in a finance support, accounts, or payroll role.

-Solid understanding of accounts payable, accounts receivable, and payroll processes.

-Proficient with Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets.

-Experience with accounting software (Xero preferred).

-Strong numeracy and data-entry accuracy.

-Understanding of basic accounting and payroll principles.

-High attention to detail and ability to deliver consistent accuracy.

-Excellent time management and ability to work to tight deadlines.

-Strong communication and customer service skills.

-Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks.

-Comfortable with change, learning, and improving processes.

-Ability to handle confidential information with integrity and discretion.

-Certificate, Diploma, or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field

title: Patient Engagement Officer — Mental Health & Psychiatry
description:
(Psychologically or Pharmacy Literate, Non-Clinical Role)

Remote | Philippines | Day or Night Shift (US Coverage)



We are a US-based psychiatry practice seeking a high-judgment Patient Engagement Officer who understands mental health patients and how they respond to communication.



This role is responsible for active patient engagement — confirmations, reminders, reassurance, and recovery — not just answering messages.



You will call patients, handle yes/no responses, and prevent cancellations and no-shows by how you communicate.



If you understand that how you ask matters as much as what you ask, this role is for you.



We are a US-based psychiatry practice seeking a high-judgment Patient Engagement Officer who understands mental health patients and how they respond to communication.



This role is responsible for active patient engagement — confirmations, reminders, reassurance, and recovery — not just answering messages.



You will call patients, handle yes/no responses, and prevent cancellations and no-shows by how you communicate.Who You Are (This Matters More Than Your Resume)



You are likely someone who has:

 • A background in pharmacy, medicine, nursing, or mental health care

 • Experience interacting with anxious, depressed, ADHD, or overwhelmed patients

 • The ability to stay calm when others are distressed

 • Strong emotional intelligence

 • A natural way of communicating safety and certainty



You do not rush.

You do not over-explain.

You do not panic.

You follow through.



What You Will NOT Do (Non-Negotiable)



Ideal Backgrounds

 • Pharmacist (strongly preferred) Must have strong communication skills

 • MD or RN (non-practicing or non-clinical role)

 • Mental health care coordinator

 • Psychiatry or behavioral health experience



Personality and judgment matter more than credentials.



You Will Be Successful If You:

 • Understand that silence feels like rejection to mental health patients

 • Know how to say “I’m here and I’m handling this” — and mean it

 • Can recognize risk without escalating unnecessarily

 • Respect boundaries and escalation paths

 • Follow systems without freelancing

 • Take pride in protecting both patients and providers



Schedule & Environment

 • Full-time, remote

 • Night shift aligned with US time zones

 • Single-client role (not agency work)

 • Long-term position with growth and leadership potential



Why This Role Matters



Mental health patients are not just “customers.”

They are people placing trust in us at vulnerable 
nts.



This role protects that trust.



If you believe that the right response at the right 
nt can change a patient’s entire experience, we want to hear from you.



1. A new patient texts: “I’m really anxious and need help today.” You cannot schedule them yet. What do you say in your first reply?

 2. A patient asks, “How much is this going to cost me?” You are not allowed to discuss pricing or billing. How do you respond?

 3. You send a text to a patient and receive no reply for two hours. How do you d
 whether to follow up, wait, or escalate?

title: Remote Technical Drafter & Estimator (Bluebeam + Excel – Full-Time)
description:
ABOUT QSA



Quality Sound Alarm (QSA) is a Canadian security systems integrator based in Atlantic Canada. We design and install CCTV, intrusion detection, access control, fire alarm, nurse call, and PA systems for commercial, industrial, and healthcare clients.

You are not expected to memorize product lines. All placement logic, coverage rules, and labour standards are documented. Your role is to apply them accurately and deliver within 24 hours.

Clear documented standards and templates



Consistent weekly workload



Stable company with a collaborative remote team



HOW TO APPLY

In the Loom video, briefly cover:



The type of system



Scope of work



Devices selected



Any challenges





Please

Best,



Brittany

title: Senior Graphic Designer with Modern Skill with AI Prompting Master Skils and proven experience with Amazon
description:
ABOUT US



Since 2013, Prodigy Media has built more than brands — we've created an Amazon-native empire. With 12 years of proven success and 28+ product lines under our belt, we don't play small. Our mission? WHO WE WANT

Think like a marketer. Design like an artist. Prompt like an AI god.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Develop Scroll-Hook Videos – using After Effects, Premiere, Runway AI, etc.

WHO YOU ARE

title: Telephony Platform Lead (SIP, Vonage, Desk Phones)
description:
Company: WorkWise Auto Solutions

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Type: Full-time

Salary: Competitive, based on experience



The Project 

We're building out a multi-tenant voice communication platform for our clients, automotive workshops across Australia. End users will have flexibility: web app, mobile app, or physical desk phones for their front-desk staff.

We've chosen Vonage as our backbone. We need someone who can own the VoIP/telephony layer end-to-end: architect it, build it, and run it.





What You'll Be Doing

- Design and build our SIP trunking infrastructure using Vonage's APIs and SIP services

- Provision and configure desk phones at scale (Yealink, Polycom, Cisco, Grandstream, whatever fits)

- Integrate physical phones alongside our web/mobile softphone options into a unified system

- Manage multi-tenant voice routing, each client needs their own numbers, extensions, and call flows

- Troubleshoot call quality issues (latency, jitter, codec problems, NAT/firewall headaches)

- Work with our dev team to integrate voice into our broader platform (webhooks, call events, recordings, IVR logic via NCCO)

- Document configurations, runbooks, and provisioning processes

- Own it ongoing, this isn't a one-and-done build; you'll manage and evolve the system



What We're Looking For

   Must Have:

      - Deep experience with SIP protocol and VoIP systems

      - Hands-on work with desk phone provisioning (auto-provisioning, config files, MAC-based setup)

      - Familiarity with SIP trunking concepts (registration, authentication, codecs, NAT traversal)

      - Understanding of VoIP networking (QoS, firewall rules for SIP/RTP, STUN/TURN)

      - Ability to diagnose call quality issues and know your way around packet captures if needed

      - Comfortable working remotely and asynchronously with a distributed team

   

   Ideally, You Also Have:

       - Experience with Vonage APIs (Voice API, SIP trunking, Programmable SIP) or similar platforms (Twilio, Plivo, etc.)

       - Worked with PBX systems (FreePBX, Asterisk, 3CX) in the past

       - Familiarity with NCCO (Nexmo Call Control Objects) or similar call flow scripting

       - Experience in multi-tenant environments, you understand the complexity of managing voice for many clients

       - Some exposure to WebRTC for browser-based calling



About WorkWise Auto Solutions

We help automotive workshops run more smoothly with Virtual Service Advisors and smart operations tools. Voice communication is a critical piece; our clients talk to their customers constantly. We need the phones just to work.

Small team. Real problems to solve. Autonomy to do it your way.



Benefits

 - Competitive salary

 - Work from home – fully remote role

 - Ongoing professional development and career growth opportunities

 - Opportunities for advancement within the company

 - A supportive and collaborative team environment



To Apply

 Follow this form, thoroughly read, and go through the steps:

   
  



This is a build-it-then-run-it role. If you like owning infrastructure, we'd like to talk. ????

title: Paid Ads Operator (Meta / Google / LinkedIn) – Lead Generation Campaigns
description:
About the Role



We are a U.S.-based agency focused on generating qualified inbound leads and booked sales conversations for service businesses.



We are looking for a Paid Ads Operator who can manage, monitor, and optimize advertising campaigns across platforms such as Meta Ads, Google Ads, and LinkedIn Ads.



This role focuses on performance-driven lead generation campaigns, not just ad posting.



You will work closely with our growth strategy and sales team to ensure campaigns generate qualified conversations and measurable results.



This position requires daily overlap with U.S. Eastern Time (EST) for 3–4 hours.



Core Responsibilities

Campaign Setup & Management



• Build and launch campaigns across Meta Ads, Google Ads, and LinkedIn Ads

• Create structured campaign architecture and naming conventions

• Set budgets, bidding strategies, and audience targeting

• Maintain organized ad account structures



Performance Monitoring



• Monitor campaigns daily for performance changes

• Optimize bids, budgets, and targeting based on results

• Pause underperforming ads and scale winning campaigns

• Maintain campaign health and efficiency



Testing & Optimization



• Test ad creatives, headlines, and messaging

• Test new audience segments and targeting strategies

• Identify opportunities to improve conversion rates and cost per lead



Lead Tracking & Landing Pages



• Ensure ad traffic routes to the correct landing pages

• Monitor lead form performance and conversion rates

• Coordinate with the team to improve landing page performance



Reporting



• Track campaign metrics and performance data

• Maintain organized reporting in Google Sheets or dashboards

• Provide regular performance updates to the team



Requirements



We are looking for someone who:



• Has experience managing Meta Ads, Google Ads, or LinkedIn Ads

• Understands B2B and lead generation funnels

• Can analyze campaign metrics such as CTR, CPC, CPL, and conversions

• Is organized and detail-oriented

• Can manage multiple campaigns simultaneously



Must be available for 3–4 hours of overlap during U.S. Eastern Time (EST).



Important – Application Instructions



1?? Your LinkedIn profile link



2?? Links or screenshots of ad campaigns you have managed

title: Product Assistant Role
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly meticulous and organised Product Assistant to join our busy team.

In this critical role, you will be the backbone of our product offerings. You will be responsible for ensuring all our amazing travel packages are accurately loaded, maintained, and priced in our systems. Your work will directly impact what our customers see and book, making your attention to detail paramount to our success.

You will be working with our internal booking system and Google Sheets daily.



Key Responsibilities

- Accurately upload new travel packages, including descriptions, inclusions, and images, into our system.

- Maintain and update existing travel packages with new travel dates, itinerary changes, and stop-sells.

- Carefully review and interpret supplier contracts to extract key information (e.g., rates, blackout dates, terms).

- Upload and manage complex pricing structures, ensuring 100?curacy.

- Conduct regular checks and audits to ensure all live pricing and availability are correct and up-to-date.

- Liaise with travel service providers to assist in uploading new travel packages and maintaining existing ones.

- Manage and organise product data using Google Sheets.

- Assist the wider product and operations team with various administrative tasks as required.



About You



To be successful in this role, you must be a systems-oriented person who thrives on accuracy.



? Non-Negotiables (Must-Haves):

- Exceptional Attention to Detail: You must be meticulous, spotting errors that others might miss. This is the most important quality for this role.

- Google Sheets Proficiency: You are highly confident working with spreadsheets, including data entry, basic formulas, and data filtering.

- Excellent English Skills: You have strong written and verbal communication skills.

- An Organised Mindset: You can manage multiple tasks and prioritise effectively in a fast-paced environment.



? Bonus Points (Nice-to-Haves):



Travel Industry Experience: A background in the travel industry (especially Australian travel) is a huge plus!

Australian Business Experience: Familiarity with working for an Australian-based company.

Systems Experience: Any previous experience with booking systems or databases is highly regarded.

title: Senior U.S. Tax Analyst (Trial with Full-Time Opportunity)
description:
Company:Harvest AF Financial Consulting

Location: Remote

Hours:nFull Time (U.S. business hours preferred)

Compensation: $10 – $20+ per hour depending on experience



---



About Our Firm



Harvest AF Financial Consulting is a Faith based U.S.-tax advisory firm that helps entrepreneurs, consultants, and small business owners legally reduce their taxes and build long-term financial strategies.



Our firm focuses on tax planning, strategy, and advisory, not just tax preparation.



We work with clients across the United States and frequently help them implement strategies such as:



• S-Corporation optimization

• retirement tax strategies

• real estate tax strategies

• depreciation planning

• business deduction optimization

• entity structuring



We are expanding our tax department and are looking for a **strong U.S. tax professional who can become a key member of our team.**



---



Important — Trial Period



This role begins as a paid trial engagement (30–60 days).



The purpose of the trial is to ensure the role is a good fit for both sides.



If the candidate demonstrates strong technical ability, communication, and reliability during the trial period, the position will transition into a long-term full-time role with our firm.



We are looking for someone who wants to grow with the company and help build the tax department over time.



---



The Role



This is not a basic tax preparation role.



This position will work closely with the founder to help analyze tax returns, identify tax planning opportunities, and support advisory work.



Responsibilities include:



• reviewing U.S. tax returns (1040, 1120S, 1065)

• analyzing Schedule C businesses and real estate activity

• identifying potential tax planning opportunities

• researching IRS tax rules and tax strategies

• assisting with tax projections and planning analysis

• reviewing depreciation schedules

• supporting tax strategy discussions before client meetings

• helping improve internal tax workflows and systems



Over time, this person may also assist in training junior preparers as the department grows.



---



Required Experience



Applicants must have real experience working with U.S. tax returns.



Strong candidates typically have experience with:



• Form 1040

• Schedule C

• Schedule E

• Form 1120S

• Form 1065

• depreciation schedules

• business deductions



Experience working with a U.S. CPA firm or U.S. tax firm is highly preferred.





Preferred Credentials



Strong candidates may have one or more of the following:



• CPA (Philippines)

• Enrolled Agent (EA)

• EA candidate

• several years working with U.S. CPA firms





Software Experience



Experience with U.S. tax software such as:



• ProConnect

• ProSeries

• Drake

• UltraTax

• Lacerte

• TaxSlayer



Experience with QuickBooks Online and Excel is also helpful.



---



Communication Skills



Because this role may eventually assist with client conversations, candidates must have:



• strong spoken English

• professional communication skills

• ability to explain financial concepts clearly



---



Who This Role Is Perfect For



This role is ideal for someone who:



• enjoys analyzing tax returns

• enjoys researching tax strategies

• wants to grow into a tax advisory role

• wants to be part of building a strong tax department



**If you only prefer data entry or basic tax preparation, this position will not be the right fit.**





Application Instructions



To apply, please submit:



1?? Your resume

2?? A short video introduction (1–2 minutes)

3?? Your answer to the following question:



A client earns $200,000 from a Schedule C consulting businessnand has two children.



What tax strategies might you explore to potentially reduce their tax liability?



We are not looking for perfect answers — we want to see how you think about tax. 



---



Our Culture



At Harvest AF Financial Consulting we value our faith and using our business as a financial ministry. We also value:



• professionalism

• strong analytical thinking

• curiosity and continuous learning

• integrity and client service







We aim to provide high-level tax advisory services that go beyond traditional tax preparation.





**Please begin your application with the words: “Harvest Tax Analyst.”**

title: Video Editor – E-Commerce Ads
description:
VIDEO EDITOR – E-COMMERCE ADS (META/FACEBOOK) | AI EXPERIENCE A PLUS

---

We are a fast-growing US-based D2C e-commerce brand selling pet products. We need a video editor who can produce direct response ads for
 and Instagram.



This is NOT a corporate video editing job. You will be editing short-form (30–90 second) ads designed to sell products. If you have experience editing e-commerce ads, product demo videos, or direct response marketing content, you are exactly who we are looking for.



? READ THIS BEFORE YOU APPLY:

We have extremely high expectations. This job is not for everyone. You will carry heavy responsibility – the ads you produce directly determine how fast this brand grows. You will be judged on output and results, not hours logged. You must think independently, solve problems without hand-holding, and take full ownership of your work. If you need micromanagement, do not apply.

That said – if you are a high performer, you will be rewarded like one. We operate a generous performance bonus system where your pay increases based on how well your ads perform. Top editors on this team will earn significantly more than the base rate. We want people who are hungry, take pride in their craft, and want to grow with a brand that is scaling fast.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:

• Edit 30–90 second direct response video ads for Meta 
/Instagram)

• Work from creative briefs provided by the founder – you will always know exactly what to produce

• Match visuals to voiceover audio precisely – what the viewer sees must match what they hear

• Use AI video/image generation tools (Sora, VEO, Higgs Field, etc.) to create B-roll footage when needed

• Deliver polished, scroll-stopping ads with clean pacing, text overlays, and transitions

• Hit deadlines – we move fast and need consistent output



REQUIREMENTS:

• Previous experience editing e-commerce or direct response ads (send portfolio/examples)

• Strong understanding of pacing, hooks, and what makes someone stop scrolling

• Good English communication – you need to understand briefs and ask questions when unclear

• Reliable internet and your own editing setup (Premiere Pro, After Effects, CapCut, DaVinci – any professional tool)

• Willingness to learn and take feedback – we will train you on our specific style

• Available minimum 4–6 hours per day



NICE TO HAVE (NOT REQUIRED):

• Experience with AI video generation tools (Sora, VEO 3, Higgs Field, Kling, etc.)

• Experience in the pet niche or health/wellness D2C brands

• Understanding of
 ad creative strategy (hooks, CTAs, UGC-style editing)



COMPENSATION:

Base pay: $800–$1,200/month USD depending on experience. We run a performance bonus system tied directly to ad results. When your ads generate strong ROAS and scale, you earn bonuses on top of your base. This is not a token gesture. Editors who consistently produce winning ads will earn significantly above the base rate. We want people who are motivated by results and want their income to grow as the brand grows.



PREFERRED WORKING HOURS:

12:00 PM – 12:00 AM Manila time (PHT). You do not need to work this entire block, but your active hours should fall within this range for communication overlap.



HOW TO APPLY:

title: Senior Meta Ads Media Buyer – Home Improvement (Full A–Z Managing)
description:
About Us



We are a fast-growing US-based performance marketing agency working exclusively with home improvement companies:



Window & door replacement, Bath remodeling, Roofing, Exterior remodeling, General contractors



We have clients.

We have infrastructure.

We have backend systems and CRM in place.



What we need now is a true A+ performance media buyer who can take full ownership of paid acquisition from A–Z.



This is not a support role.



This is a leadership-level performance role.



The Role



You will own the entire paid acquisition process for our home improvement clients.



That includes:



Creative strategy



Ad development



Campaign structure



Testing



Optimization



Scaling



Backend account organization



Performance reporting



You are responsible for results.



What “A–Z Ownership” Means



You will:



1?? Creative & Messaging Strategy



Develop hooks, angles, and offers



Write ad copy (direct response focused)



D
 what creatives need to be produced



Brief editors/designers if needed



Create variations for systematic testing



We are not producing creatives for you.



You d
 what gets made and why.



2?? Campaign Setup & Structure



Build campaigns from scratch



Implement proper testing frameworks



Structure cold, warm, and retargeting campaigns



Implement audience strategies for local markets



Install and verify tracking (Pixel, CAPI, etc.)



3?? Testing & Optimization



Launch multiple variations weekly



Test hooks, CTAs, offers, formats



Kill losers quickly



Scale winners intelligently



Improve CPL while maintaining lead quality



You must understand:



First 3-second hook psychology



Homeowner buying intent



Financing psychology



Urgency & offer positioning



4?? Performance & Scaling



Maintain target CPLs



Improve booked appointment rate



Manage budget increases without volatility



Manage multiple client accounts simultaneously



Spot creative fatigue early



You will be judged on:



Cost per qualified lead



Cost per booked appointment



Stability of performance



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (Non-Negotiable)



You must have:



3+ years paid media experience



Direct experience in home improvement OR local contractor lead generation



Managed $20k+/month in ad spend



Proven results generating qualified homeowner leads



Experience building campaigns from scratch



If you have only assisted other media buyers or followed SOPs, this is not the role.



We are looking for a performance operator.



Platforms You Must Master



Meta Ads Manager (Primary)



 Pixel & Conversion API



Retargeting & funnel sequencing



Creative testing frameworks



Bonus:



Google Search for local services



YouTube Ads



GoHighLevel familiarity



What Makes You an A+ Fit



You think in data, not opinions



You understand contractor economics



You know what homeowners respond to



You move fast and make decisions confidently



You take ownership instead of waiting for instructions



We do not micromanage.



We expect results.



Compensation



Competitive base pay (based on experience)



Performance bonuses tied to results



Long-term growth path



Opportunity to become Head of Paid Media



If you produce, you grow.



How to Apply (Serious Applicants Only)



Put “A–Z Media Buyer – Home Improvement” in your subject line.



Answer these questions clearly:



• What home improvement niches have you personally generated leads for? Include budgets and CPLs.



• Describe your launch strategy for a new window replacement client in a competitive metro market.



• If CPL increases 35% in 7 days, what is your exact diagnostic process?



• What is the biggest mistake agencies make when running ads for contractors?



Attach proof of results (screenshots with sensitive information blurred).



Include the word “CONTRACTOR” at the top of your application to confirm you read everything.



Applications that do not follow instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Executive Operations & Systems Assistant
description:
Executive Operations & Systems Assistant



MatterCorp Automation — Remote (Philippines) — Full Time

$800–$1,000/month | 40 hours/week | U.S. Central Time (8 AM–4 PM CT)



About This Role

I run a legal tech consulting practice called MatterCorp Automation. After 18+ years in enterprise IT (AT&T, MasterCard), I moved into the legal space and now help law firms systematize operations. The business is growing — and I need the right person beside me to make sure nothing falls through the cracks.



This is not a typical virtual assistant role. Your job is not just to complete tasks. Your job is to build systems that ensure important things never get missed — and then make sure those systems actually run.



If you naturally think in processes, enjoy organizing chaos, and get real satisfaction from asking: “what system should exist so this never happens again?” — this role was written for you.



Success in 30 days looks like:



Inbox triage rules + response templates in place



A task system capturing 100% of open commitments



A daily 15-minute video standup running smoothly



A simple prevention system for deadlines/follow-ups (so misses don’t repeat)



What You’ll Do



Your core function is to be my accountability partner and operational backbone.



Own my calendar — not just scheduling; ensure meetings have context and prep so I know exactly why I’m there.



Own my inbox — triage, categorize, draft responses, and ensure nothing important gets buried (after we align on rules + templates).



Run a daily 15-minute video standup every morning — we align priorities, surface what’s slipping, and you hold me accountable. Non-negotiable.



Track every open commitment across clients, projects, and personal items — and follow up relentlessly. When something is dropped, you say so directly.



Document systems as we build them — I move fast; you capture, organize, and make everything findable.



Handle research and follow-through on delegated items (travel, vendors, client materials, deliverables).



When something gets missed or falls apart, your instinct should already be forming the fix — not just the cleanup.



Where This Goes



This role starts with me — but what you’re growing into is process/system building that can extend into client operations. Strong performers can grow into an Operations Lead / Chief of Staff path.



What I Expect



Microsoft Teams is primary. Zoom is used for recorded meetings.



I’ll teach you Kanban/Agile — you don’t need it now, but you must want to learn.



Full 8-hour days aligned to U.S. Central Time (night shift PH). Night shift experience preferred.



You must be fully available during scheduled hours (no overlapping jobs/shifts during 8–4 CT).



Direct communication style; we keep things straightforward.



I’m looking for someone who commits for at least 1–2 years.



Requirements



2+ years supporting a founder/executive remotely or 2+ years in operations/project coordination



Strong systems thinking: fix the root cause, not just the instance



Respectfully direct: you can push back and surface problems early



Strong written English (you’ll draft
s to attorneys/clients)



Reliable equipment: working camera, stable internet, quiet workspace (daily video standup)



Backgrounds that tend to work well: operations assistant, project coordinator, EA in a small company, customer success ops



Compensation



$800–$1,000/month based on experience — range is firm



Weekly pay for the first month, biweekly after that



30-day check-in and 90-day evaluation; if we continue, we discuss comp based on demonstrated ownership of systems



13th-month pay (pro-rated) provided



All software/tools provided



Healthcare support is a goal I’m working toward (not guaranteed yet)



I’m looking for someone who wants a long-term working relationship, not short-term gigs. Stable work and room to grow.



How to Apply (required)



Include all items below or your application will not be reviewed:



Put “structure creates calm” at the top of your application.



Answer in writing:



What executive/operational responsibilities have you managed? Tools used, what a typical day looked like, and one time something went wrong + how you handled it.



Tell me about a time you pushed back on a boss/client. What happened and what was the outcome?



Record a 2–3 minute video (Loom/Drive/YouTube unlisted) answering:

“It’s Monday morning. Your boss has 47 unread
s, three client calls he hasn’t prepped for, a flight to book for next week, and he missed a deadline last Friday. He’s panicking. What do you do first and why?”

Walk through priorities step-by-step. I want to see how you think under pressure.



State your salary expectation (within the posted range) and earliest start date.

(Optional but recommended) If you used AI to help with your application, say how you used it.



Applications missing the passphrase or the video will not be reviewed. No exceptions.

C1 or C2 English Only

title: Sales Development Representative (SDR) – Lead Qualification & Appointment Setting
description:
SDR / Conversation Specialist

About the Company



Stremeline is a U.S.-based growth and marketing agency that helps service businesses generate qualified leads and sales conversations through structured outbound systems and paid advertising.



Our team focuses on building scalable outreach systems using cold
, LinkedIn outreach, and paid ads to consistently create new business opportunities for our clients.



Role Overview



This role focuses on converting outreach responses into qualified sales conversations.



The primary responsibility is managing prospect engagement once outreach has started and guiding interested prospects toward scheduled discovery calls.



This role plays a key part in turning outreach activity into real business opportunities by ensuring that conversations with prospects are handled promptly, professionally, and effectively.



Candidates must be available for 3–4 hours of daily overlap with U.S. Eastern Time (EST) to coordinate with the internal team.



Core Responsibilities

Inbox Monitoring



• Monitor responses from LinkedIn outreach, cold
 campaigns, and other outbound channels

• Respond promptly to inbound messages from prospects

• Manage conversations across LinkedIn,
, and messaging platforms



Prospect Qualification



• Identify whether prospects meet defined Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) criteria

• Ask basic qualifying questions when necessary

• Determine if the prospect is a good fit for a discovery call



Conversation Management



• Respond to prospects using approved messaging frameworks

• Handle basic questions and simple objections

• Maintain professional and helpful communication with prospects

• Guide conversations toward the next step in the sales process



Meeting Scheduling



• Guide qualified prospects toward scheduling a discovery call

• Book calls using the scheduling system (such as Calendly)

• Confirm appointments and ensure accurate scheduling



CRM and Reporting



• Update CRM records with conversation notes and prospect information

• Maintain accurate records of prospect engagement

• Share feedback on common objections or messaging patterns to improve outreach performance



Profile Fit



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys communication, persuasion, and helping conversations move forward.



The right candidate is responsive, organized, and comfortable interacting with potential clients through written communication.



Strong written English and the ability to handle professional conversations are essential for this role.



Experience with sales conversations, appointment setting, or SDR work is preferred.



Top Skills Required



• Written sales communication and conversation management

• Lead qualification and appointment setting

• CRM management and prospect tracking



To apply, please include the following in your application:



1?? Your LinkedIn profile link

2?? Your experience with sales conversations or appointment setting

3?? The tools you have used (LinkedIn, CRM, outreach tools, etc.)

title: UGC, TikTok Shop Influencer and Affiliate Expert
description:
ob description

Experience with TikTok Shop backend operations is a plus, but the core of this role is affiliate growth and performance management.

- Monitor performance metrics (clicks, sales, ROI, conversions, etc.).

You’ll Be a Great Fit If You:

- Enjoy analyzing data and optimizing for better performance.

- Have strong communication skills and are comfortable reaching out to creators.

Perks

title: Amazon Operations Executive
description:
Job Post — Amazon Operations Specialist

(Full-Time, Remote • Philippines)



Post this on OnlineJobs.ph. This is an execution role with real accountability — not a “checker.”



About the role

We run high-volume Amazon marketplaces and need a full-time operator to keep inventory in stock, shipments clean, and inbound discrepancies recovered. You will own the daily ops queue, work with our team, and keep everything tracked.



Schedule: Full-time (40 hrs/week).

Time zone: Overlap with Pacific Time is required.



What you will do (core responsibilities)



• Replenishment execution: Monitor Days of Cover on priority SKUs, create replenishment tickets, coordinate with warehouse/3PL, and track inbound through check-in.

• Inbound tracking: Maintain shipment tracker (working ? shipped ? delivered ? receiving ? checked-in).

• Receive discrepancy recovery: Reconcile shipped vs received by SKU, compile evidence packs, open cases, and follow up until reimbursed or resolved.

• Marketplace health queue: Address stranded inventory, search suppressed listings, pricing errors, coupon status, broken variations, image issues, and content gaps (titles/bullets).

• Case management: Write clean cases with evidence, track SLAs, escalate properly, and maintain a case log.

• Weekly reporting: Revenue-at-risk (stockouts/suppressions), what you fixed, what’s pending, blockers, and next actions.



KPIs (how you’ll be measured)



• Priority SKUs: No preventable stockouts (you escalate early).

• Inbound: 100% shipments tracked end-to-end with key dates.

• Claims: No missed claim windows; consistent follow-up until closure.

• Listing health: Suppressions/critical issues kept low and resolved quickly.

• Communication: Clear daily updates and weekly summaries; no surprises.



Requirements (do not apply if you don’t have these)



• 2+ years hands-on Seller Central experience (inventory, cases, listings).

• Strong understanding of FBA inbound workflows (shipments, receiving, discrepancies).

• Ability to build and maintain trackers (Google Sheets) with clean data discipline.

• Excellent written English for Amazon cases (clear, concise, evidence-driven).

• Reliable internet and ability to work independently without constant supervision.



Nice to have



• Experience with flat files, variation repairs, A+ content updates.

• Experience managing US and CA marketplaces.

• Experience with 3PL coordination and outbound shipment scheduling.



Application instructions (filter out weak applicants)



Reply with:

- A short summary of your Amazon operations experience (marketplaces, size of catalog).

- Your exact experience with FBA receive discrepancies (what evidence you use, how you follow up).

- A sample case message you would send to Amazon for missing units.

- Your internet speed and your available working hours overlapping with Pacific Time.

- Put the code word “CLEAN-OPS” in the first line so we know you read the post.



Compensation

Competitive for the Philippines market, based on experience. We reward operators who close loops and protect revenue.



If you’re the type who just “checks things,” don’t apply. If you can diagnose issues, run tickets, and close cases fast — apply.

title: Video Editor for 1-Minute Performance Ads (Paid Per Video)
description:
We’re looking for someone ambitious, proactive, and excited to grow in a high-performance environment.



Our 8-figure e-commerce skincare brand is seeking a
 Ads Video Editor who can transform video scripts into sales machines. You strive to become one of the best at editing direct-response video ads. You want to work in a team that pushes you, where you grow and learn new things every day, while you’re rewarded for the results your videos bring.



Compensation



*Pay-per-video: $175 per fully completed and delivered video ad

*Typical output: 10–20 videos per month depending on your capacity

*Bonuses up to $5,000 per month based on ad performance

*$250 bonus for each individual ad that reaches $30,000 in ad spend

*Flexible 40h/week schedule equivalent

*Potential to advance to Video Editor Manager, leading your own team



Opportunity



Join a team of expert marketers who’ve generated over $25,000,000 in online sales. Access elite advertising and video editing training (we’ll invest in any course you need).



Responsibilities



*Edit short-form UGC ads and long-form VSLs

*Collaborate with a video editing team led by a Creative Strategist, with ongoing feedback to level up fast.

*Your work is judged by sales performance. Our team manages over $1M per month in ad spend, and stronger results unlock bigger bonuses.

*Use ad account data to make data-driven iterations: new hooks, new angles, and new video variations.

*Study winning sales videos across industries to spot patterns and apply them to your edits.



Requirements



*1+ years of experience editing direct response video ads

*Advanced proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro

*Fluent English (written and spoken) required

*Thrives under pressure and tight deadlines

*Obsessive appetite for growth, learning, and self-improvement



About Us



Founded in Italy, we are a growing skincare brand specializing in acne treatment and anti-aging solutions. Originating from the founder’s personal struggle with acne, we now primarily operate in the US. We have improved the skin of over 100,000 women, collaborated with 600 influencers, and partnered with a celebrity doctor as the face of our marketing campaigns.



Our goal is to reach 10 million customers in the next 3 years. We foster a culture of continuous learning from top experts in our field, open communication, and data-driven decision-making.



Our fast-paced environment offers endless opportunities for professional growth and the chance to make a real difference in people’s lives through skincare.

title: Senior Accountant
description:
JOB OVERVIEW



We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly analytical US Senior Accountant to support our finance operations, primarily serving US-based entities. This role plays a critical part in ensuring accurate financial reporting, strong internal controls, and timely close processes in compliance with US GAAP.



The ideal candidate is hands-on, process-driven, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.



Role Summary

The US Senior Accountant will own key areas of the general ledger, lead month-end close activities, support audit readiness, and partner cross-functionally with Finance, FP&A, and business stakeholders. This role requires strong technical accounting expertise, attention to detail, and continuous improvement mindset.



Key Responsibilities

*General Ledger & Close

Own and manage assigned GL accounts, ensuring accuracy and completeness

Lead monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes for assigned entities

Prepare and review complex journal entries, including:

Accruals and deferrals

Revenue and expense adjustments

Depreciation and amortization

Straight-line rent

Reclassifications

Maintain and reconcile balance sheet accounts with proper supporting schedules

Perform bank reconciliations and investigate reconciling items



*Financial Reporting & Analysis

Prepare monthly financial packages and supporting schedules

Perform flux and variance analysis with clear business explanations

Support management reporting and ad hoc financial analysis

Ensure proper cutoff and period-end accuracy



*Technical Accounting & Compliance

Ensure compliance with US GAAP and company accounting policies

Support implementation and maintenance of internal controls (SOX exposure preferred)

Review transactions for proper accounting treatment

Maintain audit-ready documentation and workpapers

Support external and internal audit requests



*Fixed Assets & Multi-Entity

Manage fixed asset accounting (capitalization, depreciation, disposals, impairment)

Support multi-entity and intercompany transactions and reconciliations

Handle multi-currency accounting and FX gain/loss entries



*Cross-Functional Partnership

Act as Point of Contact (POC) for assigned entities or business units

Partner closely with AP, AR, FP&A, Payroll, and Operations

Collaborate with auditors and external partners

Support finance transformation and system improvements



*Process Improvement

Identify opportunities to automate and streamline accounting workflows

Support initiatives to accelerate the close (e.g., 15-day to 10-day close)

Assist in documenting accounting policies, SOPs, and onboarding materials

Contribute to ERP and system optimization projects



Required Skills & Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance

Strong working knowledge of US GAAP (required)

Minimum 4–6 years of progressive accounting experience

Demonstrated ownership of month-end close

Hands-on experience with NetSuite or large ERP systems (required)

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, etc.)

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

High attention to detail and strong organizational ability

Ability to work independently and meet tight deadlines

Excellent written and verbal communication skills



Preferred Qualifications

CPA or equivalent certification (strong advantage)

Public accounting background (Big 4 or mid-tier preferred)

SOX/internal controls experience

Experience supporting US-based companies

Multi-entity and multi-currency environment exposure

Experience in high-growth or PE-backed environments

Exposure to AU or Singapore entities (nice to have)



Application Instructions

To be considered for this position, please submit the following:

*Your application letter

*Unrestricted link to your updated resume/CV



Subject Line:

USSrAcct – YOUR FULL NAME



Only applicants who meet the required qualifications will be contacted.

title: Accounting Talent Pool (GL Accountant | Senior Accountant | Accounting Manager)
description:

Who This Pool Is For

• Cross-border reporting structures

• High-performance finance teams



This pool includes potential roles such as:

• GL Accountant

• Accounting Manager



Role Overview

Depending on the client requirement and your experience level, you may take on either:

You will support organizations that value accuracy, accountability, and process improvement.



Key Responsibilities

General Ledger & Close

Accruals

Deferrals

Depreciation

Amortization

Reclassifications

Financial Analysis & Reporting

Multi-Entity & Global Support

• Support cross-functional finance operations



Process Improvement

Senior / Manager-Level Responsibilities

For more experienced professionals:

Experience Levels We Welcome

Required Qualifications

Subject Line:

AccountingPool – YOUR FULL NAME

title: Payroll Accounting Specialist
description:
Shift: Night Shift (PST)

Job Type: Full-time / Independent Contractor / Remote



Please Note

Applicants without solid U.S. payroll processing and payroll accounting experience will not be considered.

Strong payroll systems proficiency (Rippling preferred), advanced Excel skills, and hands-on experience with payroll reconciliations, audits, and compliance are required.



About the Role

We are seeking a highly capable Payroll Accounting Specialist to support accurate, timely, and compliant payroll operations for a global workforce. This role sits within the Finance team and partners closely with People, Accounting, and external vendors.



You will own end-to-end payroll processing, payroll accounting, reconciliations, audits, and reporting, while supporting select employee lifecycle activities through close collaboration with the People team.



This role is ideal for someone with a strong payroll and accounting mindset, excellent attention to detail, advanced Excel skills, and the ability to operate confidently in a fast-paced, evolving environment.



What You’ll Do

*Payroll Processing & Administration

Own end-to-end payroll processing for U.S. employees

Ensure payroll accuracy across earnings, deductions, taxes, benefits, equity, commissions, and one-time payments

Coordinate payroll calendars, cutoffs, off-cycle runs, and year-end payroll activities

Partner with payroll vendors and internal teams to resolve payroll issues and employee inquiries



*Payroll Accounting & Reconciliations

Prepare and review payroll journal entries in accordance with U.S. GAAP

Perform payroll balance sheet and expense reconciliations each pay period and month-end

Support payroll accruals, true-ups, and payroll-related estimates

Maintain payroll documentation to support audits and internal controls



*Compliance & Reporting

Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local payroll tax requirements

Support payroll audits, benefits audits, and external financial audits

Assist with W-2, 1099, and other year-end payroll filings

Maintain accurate payroll records and supporting schedules



*Systems & Data Management

Administer payroll and HRIS systems, with a focus on data integrity and controls

Partner on system testing, implementations, and payroll process improvements

Maintain accurate employee data related to payroll, compensation, and benefits



*Cross-Functional People Support

Partner with the People team on onboarding, offboarding, and employee changes impacting payroll

Support benefits and leave programs as they relate to payroll processing and deductions

Assist with headcount reporting, budgeting support, and workforce analytics



*Ad Hoc Analysis & Projects

Build payroll reports and models using advanced Excel

Support special projects related to payroll transformation, system upgrades, and process standardization



Who You Are

A clear and professional communicator

Service-oriented and responsive to employee needs

Highly detail-oriented with strong follow-through

Organized and comfortable managing recurring payroll deadlines

Proactive and effective working across Finance and People teams

Adaptable and confident in evolving environments



Required Education, Experience, and Skills

3–5+ years of experience in payroll processing and payroll accounting

Strong understanding of U.S. payroll, taxes, and wage compliance

Hands-on experience preparing payroll journal entries and reconciliations

Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, complex formulas)

Experience working with payroll systems and HRIS platforms (Rippling preferred)

Experience supporting audits and maintaining payroll controls

Ability to analyze payroll data and clearly explain variances



Preferred Education, Experience, and Skills

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

Experience supporting multi-state and international payroll

Prior experience in a high-growth or startup environment

Familiarity with payroll treatment for benefits, equity, and commissions



Application Instructions

To be considered for this position, please submit the following:

*Your application letter

*An unrestricted link to your updated resume/CV



Subject Line:

PayrollAccounting – YOUR FULL NAME



!!! Only applicants who meet the required qualifications will be contacted.

title: Senior TypeScript Developer (AI & Cloud Infrastructure)
description:
About Us 

We are Agentive (
), an AI-native delivery platform building intelligent systems that combine modern cloud infrastructure with advanced AI capabilities. Our focus is on delivering scalable, production-ready solutions that integrate automation, large language models, and real-world business workflows.



We design and deploy AI-powered applications, cloud-native systems, and high-performance APIs that help organizations operate faster, smarter, and more efficiently. Our work is built on strong engineering principles, clean architecture, and measurable outcomes.



Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced Senior TypeScript Developer in the Philippines with strong AI development experience who understands that great software starts with architecture and systems thinking, not just code.



This role is for a developer who can:

* Architect scalable, production-grade TypeScript systems

* Design AI-powered workflows using modern LLM tools

* Translate business requirements into cloud-native technical solutions

* Deploy and manage infrastructure using AWS and Cloudflare

* Integrate AI coding tools such as Claude Code into real development workflows

You will be responsible for designing, building, and optimizing AI-enabled applications across backend systems, APIs, and cloud environments.



Key Responsibilities

* Design and implement scalable backend systems using TypeScript (Node.js preferred)

* Architect AI-integrated applications using LLM APIs and structured workflows

* Build and maintain cloud infrastructure on AWS (Lambda, EC2, S3, RDS, IAM, etc.)

* Configure and manage Cloudflare services (Workers, DNS, edge deployments, caching)

* Integrate and optimize AI development workflows using Claude Code and similar tools

* Design secure REST APIs and microservices architecture

* Implement CI/CD pipelines and DevOps best practices

* Optimize performance, reliability, and cost efficiency of AI workloads

* Collaborate cross-functionally to align technical solutions with business objectives



Required Skills & Experience

* 10+ years experience in software development

* Strong expertise in TypeScript (backend-heavy experience required)

* Proven experience building AI-driven systems or LLM integrations

* Experience working with AI APIs, prompt engineering, and automation pipelines

* Hands-on AWS experience (serverless, compute, storage, networking)

* Experience with Cloudflare infrastructure and edge computing

* Familiarity with Cursor, Antigravity, Claude Code or equivalent AI-assisted coding platforms

* Strong understanding of scalable architecture and clean code principles

* Experience with Git, version control workflows, and CI/CD pipelines

* Ability to work independently and take ownership of technical decisions



What We’re Looking For

* A systems thinker who designs architecture before writing code

* A senior engineer who proposes solutions, not just executes tasks

* Someone comfortable working in AI-first environments

* A developer who values performance, security, and long-term maintainability

* Strong communicator with clear technical documentation skills



Benefits

* Work in an AI-native environment building real production systems

* Opportunity to architect and influence core technical infrastructure

* Exposure to advanced AI tooling and cloud technologies

* Long-term collaboration with growth potential

* Competitive compensation based on experience



How to Apply

Please send us the following:

* Your updated CV / Resume

* A cover letter outlining an AI-powered system you designed (architecture, challenges, and outcomes) and what AI-assisted coding platform you used and for who and how long

* Links to GitHub or relevant production projects

* A short explanation of how you’ve used AWS and Cloudflare in real deployments

* A list of AI tools you’ve worked with and how they improved your development workflow

* Your expected monthly salary (in PHP)

* Your availability (hours per week). Ability to do Part time or Fulltime



And include the word “ CloudEdge ” in your application to confirm you read the entire post.



Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Job Title Remote Revenue & Lead Management Manager (UK Property Portfolio) Location: Remote (Philippines) Working Hours:
description:
Job Title: Remote Revenue & Lead Management Manager (UK Property Portfolio)

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Working Hours: UK Business Hours

Employment Type: Full-Time Remote



About the Company

We are a UK-based property company operating a growing co-living and student accommodation portfolio across multiple cities, currently managing over 100 rooms.

Our business focuses on filling rooms quickly, maintaining high occupancy, and providing a smooth tenant onboarding process.

We are looking for a highly organised and disciplined Revenue & Lead Management Manager to handle enquiry management, lead follow-up, viewing coordination, and conversion tracking.

This is a performance-driven role focused on increasing occupancy and reducing vacancy days.







Role Overview

You will be responsible for managing all incoming enquiries from property listing platforms and coordinating the entire lead pipeline from enquiry to move-in.

You will not be conducting physical property viewings but will coordinate viewings with local hosts and ensure prospects are properly followed up until they convert.

This role requires strong organisation, fast response times, and excellent follow-up discipline.





Key Responsibilities

Respond to all property enquiries quickly across platforms such as SpareRoom, OpenRent,
, and messaging channels.

Qualify potential tenants by asking key questions and confirming suitability.

Schedule property viewings with local viewing hosts or property representatives.

Track all enquiries, viewings, and conversions in a structured pipeline sheet.

Follow up consistently with prospects who have viewed properties but have not yet confirmed.

Provide daily and weekly reporting on:

• Number of enquiries received

• Viewings scheduled

• Applications received

• Move-ins confirmed

• Current vacancy pipeline

Maintain structured communication with the business owner regarding lead pipeline and occupancy trends.





Performance Expectations

Respond to new enquiries within minutes whenever possible.

Maintain a structured pipeline of all active leads.

Ensure no enquiry is left without follow-up.

Maximise viewing attendance and conversion rates.

Support occupancy growth by maintaining strong lead engagement.





Requirements

Minimum 2–3 years experience in remote sales, lead management, leasing coordination, or property-related roles.

Excellent written and spoken English.

Strong organisational and follow-up skills.

Comfortable working during UK business hours.

Experience working with international clients (UK, US, or Australia) is preferred.

Ability to work independently and manage multiple enquiries at once.





Ideal Candidate

Highly disciplined and organised.

Process-oriented rather than personality-driven.

Comfortable working with targets and performance reporting.

Strong communication and follow-up habits.

Reliable and able to work independently without constant supervision.





Compensation

Competitive salary based on experience (up to 60,000 PH PESO) but varies 

Performance-based incentives may be offered based on conversion and occupancy improvements.





Application Requirements

To be considered, please include the following in your application and
 it to 



Previous role where you handled sales leads or customer enquiries.

Average number of leads you handled per day or week.

Your typical follow-up process from first enquiry to closing a sale.

Your experience working with international clients.

A 30 second video on how you will perform on this role (No intro or old videos) - At the start of the video you must say - For Karnani



Applications without detailed responses will not be considered.





Important

This role requires strong attention to detail and follow-up discipline. We are looking for someone who is organised, reliable, and comfortable managing a structured lead pipeline.



If you are confident managing enquiries and helping convert leads into confirmed bookings, we would like to hear from you.

title: Social Media - VA
description:
About the Role

Together CFO is a fast-growing tax strategy and fractional CFO firm serving high-net-worth business owners, physicians, and attorneys. We help our clients achieve 30–60% permanent tax reductions through sophisticated, IRS-compliant structures — and we need a sharp, proactive VA to help us grow.



This is not a typical VA role. You will be the operational backbone behind our content engine, outreach systems, and day-to-day execution. If you love social media, think in systems, and get excited about AI tools — you will thrive here.



What You'll Do

Social Media Content Creation

• Create, schedule, and publish content across LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and

• Write short-form captions, hooks, and scripts tailored to each platform's algorithm and audience

• Edit short-form video (Reels, TikToks, YouTube Shorts) using CapCut, or similar

• Design graphics, carousels, and thumbnails using Canva or Adobe tools

• Track engagement metrics and adjust content strategy based on performance data

• Research trending audio, hashtags, and formats to keep content fresh and visible



AI & Automation

• Use AI tools (ChatGPT etc.) to draft, repurpose, and optimize content at scale

• Build and manage automated workflows using tools like Zapier, Make (Integromat), or similar

• Repurpose long-form content (YouTube videos, webinars,
s) into platform-specific assets

• Stay current on emerging AI tools and proactively recommend improvements to our stack



Virtual Assistant & Operations

• Manage founder's calendar, inbox, and scheduling with minimal direction

• Coordinate outreach campaigns — track leads, follow-ups, and CRM updates

• Research prospects, compile contact lists, and support LinkedIn and
 outreach sequences

• Prepare reports, decks, and documents for client-facing and internal use

• Own projects end-to-end — you are expected to figure things out, not just execute tasks



Who You Are

• You have 2+ years of hands-on experience managing social media for a business or brand

• You are fluent in at least 3–4 major AI tools and use them daily — not occasionally

• You understand content strategy, not just content creation — you think about funnels and conversion

• You are extremely organized, self-managed, and follow through without being chased

• You write clearly and concisely — no fluff, no corporate speak

• You are comfortable working in a fast-moving environment where priorities shift

• You take ownership: if something is broken, you fix it or flag it immediately



Required Tech & Tools

You should be proficient (not just familiar) with tools in these categories:



CATEGORY TOOLS

AI Tools ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Jasper, Perplexity — at least 3

Social Media LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Studio,
 Business Suite

Video Editing CapCut, Adobe Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, or equivalent

Design Canva Pro, Adobe Express, or Figma

Automation Zapier, Make (Integromat), or n8n

Project Mgmt Notion, Asana, ClickUp, or 

CRM / Outreach GoHighLevel, HubSpot, Apollo, or equivalent

Communication Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace



Nice to Have

• Experience with financial services, consulting, or B2B professional services content

• Familiarity with YouTube long-form video editing and SEO optimization

• Basic copywriting skills — can write a cold
 or LinkedIn DM that actually converts

• Experience running paid social ads (Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads)

• Background supporting a founder or executive in a high-growth business

Ngayun, as months goes on at you get more responsibilities/roles and essential sa operations ko within the game, rate increases as well ^^

or

- Good and obedient attitude, sundalo ang role nyo sa game so syempre dapat marunong kayong makisama sakin at sa iba, mahirap na ako nagbabayad eh kayo nasusunod aba
